subject: Incorrect tomogram positioning for reconstructions in Warp
body:
I am trying to reconstruct some tomograms from FIB'd lamellae in Warp using 
.xf alignment files generated by AreTomo/IMOD. My aligned tilts/tomos turn 
out fine in AreTomo/IMOD, but my reconstructions from Warp appear devoid of 
any cellular information as if they are choosing an incorrect center for 
the reconstruction (either above or below the true center). For some 
tomograms with ice contamination, you can just start to see the faint 
appearance of crystals as you reach the end of the tomogram, which is what 
lead me to the conclusion that the reconstruction center is off. 

Is there a way to fix the tomogram positioning, similar to what is done in 
IMOD? Additionally, am I missing some additional information besides the 
.xf file to help Warp identify the proper thickness/depth to center the 
reconstruction?

Thanks,

Robert
